unmapped.human_action: Search hospitality trade publications (Imbibe, Drinks International, Nation's Restaurant News) for non-alcoholic on-trade portfolio announcements or buyer friction around NA sourcing. Check for distributor funding rounds in the NA beverage space. Test the wedge hypothesis with one or two event planners — ask whether non-alcoholic guest-experience sourcing is an actual procurement pain point.
unmapped.machine_action: Monitor hospitality trade RSS feeds for 'non-alcoholic distributor', 'NA on-trade', 'mocktail sourcing', 'sober event menu'. Track LinkedIn job postings at major wine and spirits distributors for dedicated NA portfolio or business development roles. Flag any Series A or seed funding in NA beverage distribution or on-trade procurement tooling.
unmapped.methodology_footer: Source: single Dazed Digital editorial RSS item, 2026-06-11. No search volume, sales data, or trade-side corroboration attached. Angle is structurally distinct from the vocabulary_shift issue published 2026-06-10 using the same source; that issue addressed buyer identity language; this issue addresses the supply infrastructure gap implied by that consolidation.
unmapped.uncertainty: The inference from consumer vocabulary consolidation to a hospitality B2B procurement gap is one logical step beyond what the source evidence shows. The consumer trend may be real; the distributor gap is an inference, not an observed signal from the trade side.

original_issue.falsification: Kill if: hospitality trade coverage shows NA portfolios are already a mainstream distributor offer with established on-trade players; consumer research places sober-curious caffeine behavior primarily in home or delivery contexts rather than occasion settings; major incumbents (Southern Glazer's, Republic National, or equivalent UK distributors) announce dedicated NA on-trade lines.

original_issue.thesis: The sober-curious caffeine cohort is large enough that hospitality operators now face a procurement gap — no distributor has assembled an on-trade premium non-alcoholic portfolio as a packaged menu offer, creating a B2B supply-chain opportunity twelve to eighteen months ahead of mature infrastructure.
original_issue.uncertainty: The inference from consumer vocabulary consolidation to a hospitality B2B procurement gap is one logical step beyond what the source evidence shows. The consumer trend may be real; the distributor gap is an inference, not an observed signal from the trade side.

title: The Back Bar Nobody Stocks
summary: The sober-curious caffeine cohort is large enough that hospitality operators now face a procurement gap — no distributor has assembled an on-trade premium non-alcoholic portfolio as a packaged menu offer, creating a B2B supply-chain opportunity twelve to eighteen months ahead of mature infrastructure.
